title: Travel time measures for the Strategic Road Network and local ‘A’ roads: July 2024 to June 2025
body: " Our statistical practice is regulated by the Office for Statistics Regulation (OSR). OSR sets the standards of trustworthiness, quality and value in the Code of Practice for Statistics that all producers of official statistics should adhere to. You are welcome to contact us directly by emailing congestion statistics with any comments about how we meet these standards.    On the Strategic Road Network (SRN) for the year ending June 2025, the average delay is estimated to be 11.6 seconds per vehicle per mile (spvpm), compared to free flow, a 5.5% increase on year ending June 2024.   The average speed is estimated to be 56.1 mph for the year ending June 2025, down 0.7% from June 2024.   On local ‘A’ roads for the year ending June 2025, the average delay is estimated to be 46.2 seconds per vehicle per mile (spvpm) compared to free flow. This is down 2.5% on year ending June 2024.   The average speed is estimated to be 23.6 mph for the year ending June 2025, up 1.3% on year ending June 2024.   Contact us    Road congestion and travel times     Email congestion.stats@dft.gov.uk     Media enquiries 0300 7777 878         To hear more about DfT statistical publications as they are released, follow us on X at DfTstats.   "
